title: Aureline capability-inventory registry entry
description: Boundary schema for one capability_inventory_entry_record as published by the capability-inventory registry contract. Capability inventory entries make capability state first-class across product UI, docs, CLI/headless surfaces, support exports, compatibility reports, and release artifacts. Each entry binds one capability id to (1) the surface families it may appear on, (2) a declared lifecycle_state drawn from the canonical lifecycle vocabulary, (3) the claim lanes allowed to author public statements about the capability, (4) the rollout/flag gate (if any) that must be disclosed when the capability is not universally available, (5) a public-label policy that prevents hidden/internal capabilities from leaking into public claims, and (6) an export-visibility class that prevents internal-only capability surfaces from appearing in support bundles or compatibility artifacts without explicit approval. This schema is a registry entry contract, not a runtime evaluator: it does not compute effective lifecycle state, marker narrowing, or feature-flag resolution; it freezes the metadata needed so downstream projections can fail closed when a capability is missing, mislabeled, or overclaimed.

capability_inventory_entry_schema_versio.description: Integer schema version for capability-inventory entries. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

capability_id.description: Stable, machine-readable capability id. Lowercase dot/underscore/hyphen segments only so ids are safe to log, safe in support exports, and safe in release artifacts.

capability_kind.description: Which kind of capability this inventory entry describes. Mirrors schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json capability_ref.capability_kind vocabulary.

surface_family.description: Which surface family the capability may be projected onto. This is the inventory registry's surface-family vocabulary; it intentionally aligns with the capability projection surface classes, and extends them with CLI/help and release/compatibility destinations.

owner_dri.description: Named owner DRI for the capability.

dependency_marker_ref.description: Opaque dependency-marker ref (for example cap:lifecycle:marker:...). Consumers treat this as opaque text and do not parse it.

dependency_marker_ref_set.description: Declared dependency-marker refs that must be treated as first-class disclosures when the capability is projected.

rollout_gate_kind.description: Kind of rollout gate that controls whether the capability is available or claimable on a given surface.

gate_ref.description: Opaque ref to the controlling rollout/flag row (for example a row id in artifacts/governance/experiments_register.yaml).

public_disclosure_required.description: When true, any surface that lists or claims this capability MUST disclose the gate in a copy-safe way; hiding the gate behind generic wording is non-conforming.

notes.description: Optional short operator-reviewable note about the gate.

public_label_policy.description: Policy for whether the capability may have a public label (docs/help/release-note safe naming). public_label_forbidden is used for internal-only capabilities that must not leak into public claims.

public_claim_posture.description: Whether public-truth claim surfaces may cite this capability. claimable: may be named in public claim rows with its lifecycle/disclosure posture. visible_but_nonclaimable: may appear on in-product surfaces or docs as an inspected/internal detail but MUST NOT be used as a claim anchor. forbidden: must not appear in public claim channels; only internal inspection surfaces may reference it.

export_visibility.description: Whether the capability may appear in export artifacts. public_exportable means it may appear in public-proof packets and compatibility reports; support_export_only limits visibility to support bundles; internal_redacted means it must not appear in exports.

oneOf.description: Opaque ref to the kill switch that disables the capability without requiring a code change (for example a policy-bundle key, a feature-flag id, or a runtime gate ref). Consumers treat this as opaque text and do not parse it. Null only when the capability is non-killable by design (for example a structural surface like the shell frame); the validation lane requires a non-null kill switch for capabilities that declare a non-null rollout_gate.

retirement_announced_on.description: ISO date the retirement was first announced to operators. Null when the capability is announced-deprecated without a fixed date yet.

replacement_capability_id.description: capability_id of the replacement capability when the deprecation has a forward path. Null when the deprecation has no replacement.

retirement_target_window_note.description: Reviewer-readable sentence describing the retirement window (for example 'Target removal in the milestone after the next stable cut'). Required so deprecated rows can never claim a deprecation without an actionable horizon.

retirement_metadata.description: Retirement / deprecation metadata for the capability. MUST be a non-null object when lifecycle_state is one of {deprecated, disabled_by_policy, retired}; MAY be null otherwise. The validation lane enforces this conditional shape so deprecated rows cannot drift into 'announced without an exit plan'.

drill_id.description: Stable id for this row's named failure drill, drawn from the matrix's failure_drill_id_vocabulary.

forced_input.description: Forced row mutation applied under --force-drill so the runner can reproduce the named expected_check_id from a clean tree.

expected_check_id.description: Exact check_id the runner must report under --force-drill so the drill stays loud.

actionable_next_action.description: Short reviewer-readable next action when the drill fires unexpectedly.

failure_drill.description: Named failure drill for an M1-bearing surface row. Required when m1_surface_seed_membership is true; null otherwise.

m1_surface_seed_membership.description: Marks the row as part of the M1-bearing surface coverage seed. M1-bearing rows MUST also carry a non-null failure_drill so the lane can prove omissions are caught.

surface_families.description: Surface families this capability may appear on.

lifecycle_state.description: Declared lifecycle posture for the capability inventory entry. Surfaces MUST NOT widen above the canonical effective lifecycle posture when it differs.

claim_lanes.description: Lane ids allowed to author public claims about this capability (docs/help/release/support). Empty when public_claim_posture = forbidden.

public_label.description: Public label used in docs/help/release-note copy when allowed. Null when public_label_policy = public_label_forbidden.

notes.description: Optional short reviewer-facing notes about the capability inventory entry.

allOf.description: public_label_policy = public_label_forbidden forces public_label to null.
public_claim_posture = forbidden forces claim_lanes empty and export_visibility internal_redacted.
lifecycle_state in {deprecated, disabled_by_policy, retired} forces retirement_metadata to be a non-null object.
m1_surface_seed_membership = true forces failure_drill to be a non-null object.
